title: Unit 9: Trees
description: Explore hierarchical structures, parent-child nodes, and depth-first/breadth-first tree traversals.

topics.title: 9.1 Tree Creation & Basic Terminology
topics.description: A tree consists of nodes linked by directed edges, forming a hierarchy. Terms include: Root (top), Leaf (no child), Sibling (same parent), Ancestor, Descendant.

ispo.desc: Defines class representation Node containing data, left and right references.
Allocates sample tree root and child nodes in memory.
Links parent nodes to left/right child nodes to establish tree hierarchy.
Returns root node pointer.

quiz.q: What is a node with no children called in tree terminology?

topics.title: 9.2 Binary Tree Traversal Techniques
topics.description: Visit nodes of a tree in systematic order: Inorder (Left-Root-Right), Preorder (Root-Left-Right), and Postorder (Left-Right-Root).

complexity.space: O(h) call stack where h is tree height

ispo.desc: Accepts root node of binary tree.
Defines TreeNode structure.
Recurses left subtree, then processes root node, and recurses right subtree.
Prints node value to console.

quiz.q: Which traversal order processes the root node first, before visiting left and right child subtrees?

topics.title: 9.3 Insertion in Binary Tree in Level Order
topics.description: Insert a node in a binary tree at the first available position in level order traversal (BFS) using a queue.

ispo.desc: Takes root node reference and key to insert.
Allocates queue in memory to perform level order search.
Declares temporary node temp to hold dequeued item.
Creates new TreeNode object for key if left child is vacant.
Creates new TreeNode object for key if right child is vacant.
Performs BFS traversal. Places key at first vacant left/right child slot and terminates.

quiz.q: What data structure is used to search for the insertion slot in level order insertion?

topics.title: 9.4 Maximum Height or Depth of Binary Tree
topics.description: Find the maximum height of a binary tree recursively. Height is the max depth of left/right subtrees plus 1.

ispo.desc: Takes current tree node pointer.
Allocates local variables leftDepth and rightDepth to store child tree depths.
Recursively calculates depths of left and right child subtrees.
Returns height 0 for empty tree/node.
Returns maximum child depth + 1.

quiz.q: What is the max height of a binary tree containing only the root node?

topics.title: 9.5 Deletion in a Binary Tree
topics.description: Delete a node from a binary tree by replacing its value with the deepest, rightmost node's value, and then deleting that deepest node.
topics.cheatSheet:
1. Find target node and deepest rightmost node via BFS.
2. Set target.val = deepest.val.
3. Delete deepest rightmost node.

ispo.desc: Takes root node and key value to delete.
Allocates traversal queue in memory.
Declares temp (will hold deepest node) and keyNode (points to target node).
Declares variable x to hold deepest node's value.
Uses BFS queue traversal to locate both target node and deepest node.
Copies deepest node value into target node, and calls deleteDeepest helper to prune the leaf.
Returns modified root node pointer.

quiz.q: Why is the target node's value swapped with the deepest, rightmost leaf's value during binary tree deletion?
o: To ensure the tree shrinks from the bottom and remains as compact/complete as possible
